occurrence_summary.deterministic_across_runs: same source phrase; consistent 120-char verbatim head when ellipsis present; deterministic RED across 4 runs

artifact_safety_note: quote_snippet_60 is truncated by the checker for safety; full quote text lives only in scratchpad report.md (never committed) — full text is unavailable for this inspection but the head + length + ellipsis-fragment breakdown are sufficient to classify the mechanism.

mismatch_classification.primary_evidence: 3/4 runs show ellipsis-fragment split of 120 verbatim + 34 unmatched; model concatenates a verbatim source span and a non-source tail with '...' between them.
mismatch_classification.secondary_evidence: 1/4 runs shows a 179-char continuous quote (no ellipsis, no fragment split); the tail beyond ~120 chars deviates from source — likely grammar repair, added conjunction, or paraphrase to make the quote read as a complete sentence.

rejected_categories.C_morphological: no evidence of tense/plurality/possessive change alone
rejected_categories.D_punctuation_only: would qualify for R2 tier and be AMBER; every occurrence is RED at Tier 1-3 or ellipsis-fragment tier
rejected_categories.E_whitespace_unicode: checker already normalizes curly quotes, dashes, ellipsis, pipe-newline — mismatch survives normalization
rejected_categories.H_source_selection_mismatch: company_check pass; role_check pass; cited jd_id = jd_000201; quoted head IS from jd_000201 body
rejected_categories.I_appendix_citation_error: appendix entry for jd_000201 present and consistent (NVIDIA · Senior AI ML Solution Engineer)

lineage.value_form: body inserted verbatim into user message
system prompt + user message streamed to Anthropic
streamed markdown; writes Evidence quote inside double quotes
lineage.mutation_risk: ROOT-CAUSE STAGE — model paraphrases / bridges non-contiguous spans / adds grammar / omits with ellipsis
none (sentinel is HTML comment, stripped client-side)

lineage.value_form: extracts quoted string; captures company + jd_id
3-tier + ellipsis + R2 substring match against record.body

lineage_conclusion: mismatch is introduced EXCLUSIVELY at the model-generation stage; all other stages are pass-through and byte-preserving; checker is faithful.

explicit_quote_wording_in_prompt: Line ~79: 'Allowed: quoting language from a specific JD in the evidence list (with the jd_id / company tag), since the user can verify it in the appendix.'
Line ~95: 'Why it\'s a gap for THIS user (what\'s missing from THIS resume — quote a phrase if useful)'
Line ~97: 'One evidence quote from a real JD (give the company)'
explicit_quote_wording_absent: no 'quote may be a fragment' allowance
no 'verify quote appears in supplied text' self-check
no explicit output shape separating quote token from interpretation

output_format_findings.current_shape: single Markdown bullet under each gap: '- One evidence quote from a real JD (give the company)'
output_format_findings.quote_and_explanation_blur: quote lives in the same free-form bullet as the surrounding gap prose; no structural boundary forces exact copying

prompt_findings.prompt_side_matches_checker_regex: checker recognizes this specific pattern; the prompt itself does NOT instruct the model to produce exactly this pattern — it emerges from example-following

options.summary: Add an explicit verbatim/no-repair contract to the report system prompt
Instruct model to output distinct 'Quote:' and 'Interpretation:' lines under each gap
options.implementation_complexity: low prompt + medium checker/UI parser tolerance verification
options.compatibility_with_existing_format: medium (Markdown structure changes; checker regex needs to still match)
options.migration_risk: medium (parser update if UI/harness relies on current shape)

options.summary: Model emits JSON {job_id, quote, interpretation}; render Markdown separately server-side
options.implementation_complexity: high (streaming JSON + server render + client update + harness)

options.summary: Model outputs (jd_id, start_index, end_index); server slices exact source text
options.implementation_complexity: high (span extraction reliability; model must correctly locate offsets)

options.summary: Best-effort map near-match quotes back to source
options.risk_of_concealing_hallucination: HIGH — silently rewrites model output to look faithful

options.summary: Loosen R1 to fuzzy / accept the current mismatch shape
options.recommendation: REJECT (do not add edit-distance / fuzzy / LLM judge)

recommended_design.secondary_composable: B_separate_quote_and_interpretation_fields (adopt only if A alone does not close jd_000201 in a paid A/B validation loop)
recommended_design.rationale: Smallest surface, highest auditability, zero checker/R1/R2 impact, matches user's minimum-first anti-overengineering preference. If A alone insufficient after paid A/B, escalate to B in a follow-up loop.
recommended_design.checker_impact: zero — R1 and R2 remain unchanged; success is measured by RED disappearing at the model output, not by loosened checker
recommended_design.ui_parser_impact: zero for A; minor Markdown-format tolerance check for B

proposed_prompt_patch.insertion_point: immediately after 'company-specific-claim rule' block and before '## Target role' section
proposed_prompt_patch.patch_wording:
CRITICAL — evidence-quote-verbatim rule (read twice):
- Every quoted Evidence span MUST be copied verbatim from ONE contiguous span of the cited job description.
- Do NOT paraphrase, repair grammar, change tense, change plurality, add or remove articles, add or remove conjunctions, or combine separated fragments inside quotation marks.
- A quote does not need to be a complete sentence. Grammatical fragments are allowed and preferred over stitched-together spans.
- If the useful source text is grammatically incomplete, quote the incomplete source text EXACTLY and put your explanation OUTSIDE the quotation marks.
- If you would need '...' inside a quote to skip source text, do NOT do that; either quote the first contiguous span alone or two separate quotes.
- Before finalizing the report, verify that every quoted string occurs EXACTLY (character-for-character) in the supplied text for the cited jd_id. If it does not, shorten or replace it with an exact source span.
proposed_prompt_patch.output_shape_note: no Markdown-shape change; existing 'Evidence quote: "TEXT" — Company, jd_XXXXXX.' checker regex remains untouched

stages: Implement minimal prompt patch in src/lib/prompts.ts (no other file changes)
Deterministic tests: prompt string assertions (patch text present)
Run Fixture A exactly once (no retries)
Run Fixture B exactly once (no retries)
Compare: legacy verdict · report structure · appendix presence · QI verdict · jd_000201 result · new R1 failures · R2 fires · appendix uncited telemetry
success_criteria: jd_000201 no longer R1 RED (either matches verbatim / normalized / case-insensitive, or is replaced by a shorter exact quote)
no new fabricated / unmatched quotes appear in A or B
legacy 25-check report remains structurally valid (all post-A/B checks pass)
failure_criteria: R1 merely hidden by format/parser change
quotes become longer or more hallucinated
evidence loses usefulness (all quotes become 4-word fragments)
new unmatched quotes appear in previously-clean fixtures

deterministic_tests_available_dollar_0: prompt string assertion: 'evidence-quote-verbatim rule' substring exists in reportSystemPrompt output
output-schema unit test: 'Evidence quote: "..." — Company, jd_XXXXXX.' regex still recognized by checker
known jd_000201 exact-span fixture: source_phrase_head_120 appears in web_bundle.json record body
negative example (grammar-added article) unit test: 'Evaluate and select ML approaches for THE specific problems' should fail Tier 1-3 (already covered by checker)
non-contiguous-bridging example: 'phrase A ... phrase B' where A and B come from different source spans should be RED at ellipsis-fragment tier (already covered by 5c checker)
R2 punctuation-only preservation test: existing R2 fixture stays AMBER
interpretation-outside-quotes tolerance: bullet with quote followed by free-form text still parses

open_questions.question: prompt-only fix (Option A) OR structured quote/interpretation format (Option B)?
open_questions.recommendation: start with Option A alone; escalate to A+B only if paid A/B validation shows A insufficient — matches minimum-first preference

open_questions.question: should quote spans favor shortest exact phrase OR full source sentence?
open_questions.recommendation: shortest useful contiguous span; explicitly allow fragments; discourage stitched multi-span quotes

open_questions.question: should the model perform an explicit self-check before final output?
open_questions.recommendation: yes — one-sentence instruction is cheap and directly addresses the observed non-contiguous bridging

open_questions.question: should implementation alter only prompt text OR also Markdown structure?
open_questions.recommendation: prompt text only (Option A); preserve existing 'Evidence quote:' Markdown shape so checker regex is untouched

open_questions.question: should A+B paid validation be required before any promotion-related work?
open_questions.recommendation: yes — do NOT start AgentOps-5f-promote until at least one full paid A+B validation loop shows the patch works without new failures
